name: Does ChatGPT work better for lawyers than me for my own case research?
description: Lawyers use it better with skills; both need verification.[1]

mainEntity.name: Does ChatGPT work better for lawyers than me for my own case research?

acceptedAnswer.text: Lawyers use it better with skills; both need verification.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Lawyers use it more effectively with precise prompts and verification, but neither replaces professional research.[1][2] Lay users risk higher errors without legal training.[1] Key factors: Role assignment in prompts; context provision; human oversight. Learn basic prompting but always verify with counsel. It's a tool, not advisor.
